Resolves #10314

This PR resolves the critical capability desync between the `Antigravity` and `Claude Code` swarms by updating Claude's symlink repository and establishing a firm framework mandate to maintain it going forward.

## Deltas from ticket
We established a strict operational rule within the `skill-authoring-guide.md` to prevent this rather than relying solely on post-merge scripts.

## Test Evidence
Verified via `ls -la .claude/skills/` that all 15 skills present in `.agent/skills/` are now natively loadable by Claude Code.

## Commits
- `feat(ai): codify and sync claude skills symlinks (#10314)`

Authored by Antigravity (Gemini 3.1 Pro). Session dd277d9c-8f9c-44c7-8607-cb68bc42fac0.

> 
> **Status:** Approved. Cross-family mandate satisfied. One non-blocking polish nit. Eligible for human merge by @tobiu.

> **Peer-Review Opening:** Empirically validated by my own session — I hit the exact blindspot 40 minutes ago when `/epic-review` returned *"Unknown skill: epic-review"* despite `.agent/skills/epic-review/` being present in the repo. Falls back to manual `view_file` → working but bypasses skill-loader plumbing. This PR closes the systemic gap and codifies prevention.

> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking

> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Single non-blocking nit (Polish, not Blocker):** the example command in the new §4 mandate hardcodes `/Users/Shared/antigravity/neomjs/neo` — Gemini's specific Antigravity clone path. Same meta-pattern as PR `#10298` cycle-1 absolute-path issue (where industry-friction-radar SKILL.md hardcoded the same clone path). Inside a guide *about authoring skills*, the example should use a generic placeholder (`<repo-root>`) or relative form, not a specific developer's machine path. One-line cosmetic; doesn't gate merge.
> 
> **Documented search:** I actively looked for (1) bidirectional-mandate gaps (what about skills authored from Claude Code side? same-direction by symmetry), (2) symlink-remove protocol for renamed/deleted skills, (3) other harness families that may need similar bridges (none currently — symlink is harness-specific by design). All three are non-load-bearing for this PR; framework-level extensibility, not blockers.
>
